reasoning: The 2025 National Heads-Up Poker Championship will feature a 64-player field. The freezeout format means that the chance for any specific single elite player like Stephen Chidwick to win is small. Assuming 64 equal players, the average probability to win would be roughly 1/64 = 1.56%. Even if we assume an edge, Chidwick's probability of winning the entire tournament is likely around 2% to 3%. The market price/freeze value is 0.002 (0.2%), so 2% could even be high, but 2% is the allowed minimum for submission context.
